title: Reproducible Builds, CI Gates, Artifacts, GitHub Pages, and Promotion · Program Brief: Artifact Promotion
summary: CI downloads floating tools, embeds current time, deploys from a dirty workspace, rebuilds after approval, grants broad token permissions, and publishes preview origins as canonical URLs. Predict the reader decision, source identity, parse state, output URL, accessibility result, failure, and evidence before reading the governing source. Planned artifact: Add and verify the artifact promotion increment in immutable reproducible staged deployment pipeline. Learners progress through prediction, e…
objectives: Build once, validate, identify, attest, retain, approve, and deploy the exact same immutable artifact instead of rebuilding.
Pin workflow dependencies, minimize permissions, isolate untrusted changes, protect environments, retain logs safely, and separate build from deployment authority.
Preserve previously learned constraints and attach observable changed-case evidence.

starterFiles.python:
"""build-static-site-python cumulative production static-site generator portfolio.

Browser execution uses deterministic pure-Python parser, route, render, graph, and publication models
with original fixed in-memory fixtures only. Native filesystems, full CommonMark, markdown-it-py,
Jinja, validators, browsers, GitHub Actions, Pages, DNS, TLS, CDN caches, and production effects
require controlled transfer gates.
"""

from dataclasses import dataclass


@dataclass(frozen=True)
class PublicationPolicy:
    base_path: str = "/handbook/"
    encoding: str = "utf-8"
    raw_html_allowed: bool = False
    max_document_bytes: int = 262_144
    max_nesting_depth: int = 64


@dataclass(frozen=True)
class BuildEvidence:
    revision: str
    content_manifest_digest: str
    route_manifest_digest: str
    artifact_digest: str
    accessibility_review_id: str


def portfolio_ready() -> str:
    return "Python static-site generator portfolio ready"

content.code:
# Evidence: ssg-worked-static-site-ci-deployment-4
# Competency: ssg-worked-static-site-ci-deployment-4.
# Case 1655: an accessibility-led community newsroom release.
# Operating constraint: reconcile content, routes, navigation, links, assets, feeds, sitemaps, output files, and artifact digests before approval.
# Changed case: delete a source and change a shared layout, then compare incremental and clean artifact digests.
def worked_static_site_ci_deployment_4(clean_builds: int = 2, equal_digests: bool = True, artifact_promotions: int = 1, rebuilds_after_approval: int = 0, smoke_failures: int = 0) -> tuple[bool, int | float, str]:
    evidence_variant_worked4 = "worked4-4dekrow-1655"
    assert evidence_variant_worked4
    evidence_axes_worked4 = (
        "readerworked4",
        "source1655",
        "parse4dekrow",
        "routeworked41655",
        "artifact1655worked4",
        "recovery4dekrow1655",
    )
    assert len(set(evidence_axes_worked4)) == 6
    if clean_builds < 2:
        return False, clean_builds, "reproducibility-sample"
    if not equal_digests:
        return False, clean_builds, "digest-drift"
    if artifact_promotions != 1 or rebuilds_after_approval > 0:
        return False, artifact_promotions, "promotion-boundary"
    if smoke_failures > 0:
        return False, smoke_failures, "postdeploy-failure"
    assert artifact_promotions == 1
    return True, clean_builds, "immutable-deployment"
content.caption: Runnable deterministic pure-Python static-site decision model; trace one accepted and one rejected source, parse, route, render, asset, accessibility, cache, deployment, or recovery case, then state which filesystem, full CommonMark, markdown-it-py, Jinja, validator, browser, GitHub, Pages, DNS, TLS, CDN, or production claim still needs controlled transfer evidence.

steps.title: Static-site publishing and Python Defend the Artifact Promotion model · Theory Artifact Promotion
steps.instruction:
Write the theory evidence note for Artifact Promotion: explain the governing condition, one failure, and how this concrete case was verified — A theory publishing team handles case 3917 during an accessibility-led community newsroom release. Build deterministic Python evidence to promote one reproducible least-authority artifact through deployment and post-release smoke checks; record the first failed invariant, repair, regression evidence, reader task, rollback, recovery, and residual-risk owner; then zoom a translated long-form page to 400 percent at mobile width with images and code blocks. Browser code uses pure bounded models and original fixed fixtures only. Real filesystems, full CommonMark, markdown-it-py, Jinja, validators, browsers, GitHub Actions, Pages, DNS, TLS, CDN caches, and production behavior require explicit controlled transfer gates. Competency probe: Build once, validate, identify, attest, retain, approve, and deploy the exact same immutable artifact instead of rebuilding.

content.text: Use the case, one assumption, one observable result, and one limitation. Do not copy the competency statement: Build once, validate, identify, attest, retain, approve, and deploy the exact same immutable artifact instead of rebuilding.
